prompt:
You are working on the TaskFlow Pro project at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-aWoU5K.

Review the notification system implementation in this codebase. Two previous developers may have made **conflicting architectural choices** about how services communicate for notifications.

Your task:
1. Identify any architectural conflicts in the notification approach
2. Evaluate both approaches (event-driven vs direct calls) in the context of this project
3. Choose the better approach with clear justification
4. Unify the codebase so it follows a single consistent notification architecture
5. Ensure all tests pass after unification
6. Document your decision and rationale (in code comments or coordination files)

parameters.task: Review and unify conflicting notification system architectures (event-driven vs direct calls)

parameters.prompt:
Explore the notification system in this codebase thoroughly. I need to understand:

1. All files related to notifications (search for "notification", "notify", "event", "EventBus", "EventEmitter" patterns)
2. How services communicate for notifications - look for both event-driven patterns (event bus, pub/sub, emit/on) and direct call patterns (service imports calling notification methods directly)
3. Any conflicting architectural approaches between different parts of the codebase
4. Test files related to notifications

Report file paths, key code patterns, and any conflicts you find. Be very thorough - check src/, tests/, and any config files.

parameters.description: Run all tests to see current state

cons: Adding new notification channels requires modifying multiple services
alternatives.option: Keep direct service calls pattern (Developer B's approach)
pros: Easier to debug in small codebases
alternatives.reason_rejected: Violates project architecture guidelines and creates coupling that won't scale

alternatives.option: Keep hybrid approach (both events and direct calls)
alternatives.reason_rejected: Worst of both worlds — complexity without benefit

parameters.context: Two developers made conflicting changes: one implemented pure event-driven notifications (UserService), the other added direct NotificationService calls to OrderService. CLAUDE.md mandates 'services depend on repositories, never on each other directly' and 'events are preferred over direct cross-service calls'. The codebase currently has OrderService both emitting events AND calling NotificationService directly — a redundant dual-path.
parameters.rationale: Event-driven is the right choice: (1) CLAUDE.md explicitly prescribes it, (2) UserService already follows it, (3) loose coupling means adding notification channels requires only new listeners not service modifications, (4) EventBus already provides typed events and event log for debugging. NotificationService will subscribe to EventBus events instead of being called directly.
parameters.summary: Unify notification architecture to event-driven pattern via EventBus, removing direct service-to-service calls

parameters.detail: Resolved conflicting notification architectures by choosing event-driven (EventBus) over direct service calls, per CLAUDE.md guidelines. Changes: (1) OrderService no longer depends on NotificationService — only emits events via EventBus. (2) NotificationService now takes EventBus as constructor dependency and subscribes to order:created and order:status-changed events. (3) Removed dead CallbackRegistry code (src/utils/callback-registry.ts). (4) Updated all tests to reflect event-driven pattern. Test count went from 72 to 70 (removed 2 direct-call-specific tests that are now covered by event-driven NotificationService tests).
parameters.summary: Unified notification architecture to event-driven pattern. All 70 tests pass.
